title: Support Reply
body: Write the result as a short support reply that can be sent as-is. Keep the original intent, tone direction, and stated constraints grounded in the transcript. Keep the tone calm and direct, but do not over-formalize casual wording unless the transcript already supports it. Do not invent policy, compensation, timelines, investigation results, or commitments. If the transcript does not support a specific answer, use a cautious reply that does not add new facts.
